title: Dryopteris sakuraii
taxonomy: Dryopteris sakuraii is a species of fern. It belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its frond and sporophyte. Dryopteris sakuraii is further classified as a member of the genus Dryopteris, which consists of various fern species. The classification of Dryopteris sakuraii is used in botany to identify and distinguish it from other plant species.

intro: Dryopteris sakuraii, also known as Sakurai's wood fern, is a species of fern native to Japan, characterized by its delicate fronds and compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are thought to aid in reducing symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris sakuraii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion, a decoction, or a tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
